DataRequest.Name: Excessive End User Mail / SCW
DataRequest.Description: Detects a scenario where a host, that is itself not an authorized mail gateway, is sending excessive emails (more than 20 emails in 2 minutes). This behavior may indicate malware running on an end host that is trying to send spam or privileged information to its own set of mail servers (which may be compromised).

SubPattern.name: EndUserExcessMail
SubPattern.SingleEvtConstr: ipProto=6 AND (destIpPort=25 OR destIpPort=465 OR destIpPort=587) AND srcIpAddr NOT IN (Group@PH_SYS_DEVICE_SEC_GW,Group@PH_SYS_APP_MAIL_SERVER) AND srcIpAddr IN (Group@PH_SYS_NETWORK_ENTERPRISE_INTERNAL_NET) AND eventType IN (Group@PH_SYS_EVENT_PermitTraffic,Group@PH_SYS_EVENT_DenyTraffic)

SubPattern.name: EndUserExcessMail2
SubPattern.SingleEvtConstr: ipProto=6 AND (destIpPort=25 OR destIpPort=465 OR destIpPort=587) AND destIpAddr NOT IN (Group@PH_SYS_DEVICE_SEC_GW,Group@PH_SYS_APP_MAIL_SERVER) AND eventType IN (Group@PH_SYS_EVENT_PermitTraffic,Group@PH_SYS_EVENT_DenyTraffic)
